name: A Variation on the Theme of Pashka
recipeInstructions:
1. Allow the cream cheese, butter, and egg yolks to come to room temperature for at least 2 hours.
2. In a large mixing bowl, beat the cream cheese with a wooden spoon until smooth. Add the butter and continue beating.
3. Gradually add the powdered sugar, then the egg yolks one at a time, ensuring each is fully incorporated before adding the next.
4. Stir in the vanilla extract and fold in the toasted almonds.
5. Prepare the mold by washing and drying it inside. Line the bottom with a double thickness of cheesecloth wrung out in cold water.
6. Pour the cheese mixture into the mold until full, then cover with plastic wrap.
7. Refrigerate for several hours or overnight to set properly.
8. To unmold, carefully lift the cheesecloth and invert the mold over a plate. Gently turn it upside down to release the Pashka onto the plate.
9. Trim any excess cheesecloth around the edges of the pot.
10. Garnish the top of the Pashka with whole strawberries and strawberry halves. Serve with additional crushed, sweetened strawberries as a sauce, optionally adding brandy or liqueur for flavor.
This recipe is from Newman's Own Cookbook.
